mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translate into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive return hit rate in top PAYEMS quintiles is less than 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1% suppresses risk-free returns and pushes capital into risk assets, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as long as the low-rate environment persists.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the mean forward return in that regime is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts creates a feedback loop of consumer and residential investment demand that exceeds single-sector effects, driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: A joint test of the interaction term in a regression of 20-day returns on RSAFS and HOUST yields p > 0.05, or the interaction adds less than 2% incremental R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with unemployment below 6%, accelerating industrial production signals capacity constraints and impending cost pressures that trigger multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 6%, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that precedes downward earnings revisions and negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or directional accuracy is not significantly different from 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accommodative fed funds rates and accelerating retail sales generates a liquidity-fueled demand boost that amplifies equity returns beyond either factor independently, leading to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and RSAFS is not statistically significant (t-stat < 2.0) in predicting 60-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth captures the economy's underlying momentum; positive contemporaneous growth predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns as markets price in sustained expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production steeper than 1% signals a manufacturing downturn that drags aggregate earnings expectations lower, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the short signal is above -0.2 over a 2-year window.
